Introduction

Kairo is an AI-powered CLI that brings intelligent development workflows directly to your terminal. This documentation covers everything from installation to advanced usage.

Current version
v0.1.0
License
MIT
Last updated
June 2025

Installation

Install Kairo globally using npm. Works on macOS, Linux, and Windows.

terminal
$ npm install -g kairo-cli
+ kairo-cli@latest
OK Installed successfully

Prerequisites: Node.js 18+ is required. Verify your Node version with node --version.

Quick Start

Once installed, set up your AI provider and start chatting with Kairo.

terminal
$ kairo config set provider anthropic
OK Provider set to: anthropic
terminal
$ kairo config set api_key sk-ant-...
OK API key configured
terminal
$ kairo chat
~ Kairo - I'm ready
|
| What are we building today?
~ Type your message to begin

Essential commands

kairo chatStart an AI chat session
kairo modelsList available AI models
kairo configView or update configuration
kairo helpDisplay help information

Examples

Common use cases to get you started with Kairo.

Code Review

$ kairo review src/components/button.tsx

Review a specific file for bugs, security issues, and improvement suggestions.

Debug Help

$ kairo explain

Get an explanation of the last error or exception with context from your codebase.

Architecture

$ kairo chat

Discuss design patterns, architecture decisions, and get AI-powered suggestions.

Refactoring

$ kairo fix src/utils/api.ts

Automatically apply suggested fixes and refactoring to your code.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do I need an API key?

Yes. Kairo uses your own AI provider API keys. You can configure them with kairo config.

Is Kairo free?

Kairo itself is free and open source. You only pay for the AI API usage from your chosen provider.

Can I use it offline?

Yes, with local models via Ollama or LM Studio. Set your provider to use local models.

Does Kairo store my code?

No. Your code is processed locally and sent only to the AI provider you configure. We don't store your code.

Which platforms are supported?

Kairo works on macOS, Linux, and Windows (via WSL or native).

Troubleshooting

Command not found

Ensure Node.js 18+ is installed and npm global packages are in your PATH.

API key errors

Run kairo config to verify your provider and API key settings.

Slow responses

Check your internet connection or try a different AI provider with lower latency.

Kairo

Your AI teammate in the terminal.

MIT License · 2026
